title: Jinja2 Environment Management
content:
## Environment Class

The `Environment` class is the core component of Jinja2. It manages shared variables such as configuration, filters, tests, globals, and other runtime options. Instances of this class can be modified if they are not shared and no template has been loaded yet.

### Purpose

- Manages global configuration for template rendering.
- Holds collections of filters, tests, and global variables.
- Handles template loading, parsing, compiling, and caching.
- Supports extensions for custom functionality.

### Key Exports/Functions/Props

- **`__init__(...)`**: The constructor for the `Environment` class, accepting numerous parameters to configure the Jinja environment (e.g., `block_start_string`, `extensions`, `loader`, `cache_size`).
- **`block_start_string`, `block_end_string`, `variable_start_string`, `variable_end_string`, `comment_start_string`, `comment_end_string`**: Strings defining the delimiters for blocks, variables, and comments.
- **`extensions`**: A list of Jinja extensions to be loaded.
- **`loader`**: The template loader responsible for finding and loading template files.
- **`cache_size`**: The maximum number of templates to keep in the cache.
- **`filters`**: A dictionary of available filters.
- **`tests`**: A dictionary of available tests.
- **`globals`**: A dictionary of global variables available in all templates.
- **`add_extension(extension)`**: Adds an extension to the environment after initialization.
- **`overlay(...)`**: Creates a new environment that inherits settings from the current one but allows for overriding specific attributes.
- **`parse(source, name, filename)`**: Parses template source code into an abstract syntax tree (AST).
- **`lex(source, name, filename)`**: Lexes the source code and returns a generator yielding tokens.
- **`preprocess(source, name, filename)`**: Preprocesses the source code with all registered extensions.
- **`compile(source, name, filename, raw, defer_init)`**: Compiles template source code into Python bytecode or a string representation of the code.
- **`getitem(obj, argument)`**: Safely retrieves an item or attribute from an object.
- **`getattr(obj, attribute)`**: Safely retrieves an attribute from an object.
- **`call_filter(name, value, args, kwargs, context, eval_ctx)`**: Invokes a filter on a given value.
- **`call_test(name, value, args, kwargs, context, eval_ctx)`**: Invokes a test on a given value.

### Usage Example

```python
from jinja2 import Environment, FileSystemLoader

# Create a FileSystemLoader to load templates from a directory
loader = FileSystemLoader('./templates')

# Create an Environment instance
env = Environment(
    loader=loader,
    autoescape=True, # Enable autoescaping for HTML
    trim_blocks=True, # Strip leading newlines after blocks
    lstrip_blocks=True # Strip leading whitespace before blocks
)

# Load a template
template = env.get_template('my_template.html')

# Render the template with context data
rendered_output = template.render(username='World', items=['apple', 'banana'])
print(rendered_output)
```

## Environment Class

The `Environment` class is the core component of Jinja2. It manages shared variables such as configuration, filters, tests, globals, and other runtime options. Instances of this class can be modified if they are not shared and no template has been loaded yet.

### Purpose

- Manages global configuration for template rendering.
- Holds collections of filters, tests, and global variables.
- Handles template loading, parsing, compiling, and caching.
- Supports extensions for custom functionality.

### Key Exports/Functions/Props

- **`__init__(...)`**: The constructor for the `Environment` class, accepting numerous parameters to configure the Jinja environment (e.g., `block_start_string`, `extensions`, `loader`, `cache_size`).
- **`block_start_string`, `block_end_string`, `variable_start_string`, `variable_end_string`, `comment_start_string`, `comment_end_string`**: Strings defining the delimiters for blocks, variables, and comments.
- **`extensions`**: A list of Jinja extensions to be loaded.
- **`loader`**: The template loader responsible for finding and loading template files.
- **`cache_size`**: The maximum number of templates to keep in the cache.
- **`filters`**: A dictionary of available filters.
- **`tests`**: A dictionary of available tests.
- **`globals`**: A dictionary of global variables available in all templates.
- **`add_extension(extension)`**: Adds an extension to the environment after initialization.
- **`overlay(...)`**: Creates a new environment that inherits settings from the current one but allows for overriding specific attributes.
- **`parse(source, name, filename)`**: Parses template source code into an abstract syntax tree (AST).
- **`lex(source, name, filename)`**: Lexes the source code and returns a generator yielding tokens.
- **`preprocess(source, name, filename)`**: Preprocesses the source code with all registered extensions.
- **`compile(source, name, filename, raw, defer_init)`**: Compiles template source code into Python bytecode or a string representation of the code.
- **`getitem(obj, argument)`**: Safely retrieves an item or attribute from an object.
- **`getattr(obj, attribute)`**: Safely retrieves an attribute from an object.
- **`call_filter(name, value, args, kwargs, context, eval_ctx)`**: Invokes a filter on a given value.
- **`call_test(name, value, args, kwargs, context, eval_ctx)`**: Invokes a test on a given value.

### Usage Example

```python
from jinja2 import Environment, FileSystemLoader

# Create a FileSystemLoader to load templates from a directory
loader = FileSystemLoader('./templates')

# Create an Environment instance
env = Environment(
    loader=loader,
    autoescape=True, # Enable autoescaping for HTML
    trim_blocks=True, # Strip leading newlines after blocks
    lstrip_blocks=True # Strip leading whitespace before blocks
)

# Load a template
template = env.get_template('my_template.html')

# Render the template with context data
rendered_output = template.render(username='World', items=['apple', 'banana'])
print(rendered_output)
```
